mcqs.question: Which type of attribute is computed from the children of a node to the parent node?
mcqs.explanation: Synthesized attributes are computed from the values of attributes at the children nodes and then passed up to the parent node.

mcqs.question: Which parsing technique is most suitable for S-attributed definitions?
mcqs.explanation: S-attributed definitions use only synthesized attributes, which can be efficiently evaluated during the reduce operations of LR parsing.

mcqs.question: In L-attributed definitions, inherited attributes can depend on:

options.text: Attributes of left siblings and parents
Attributes of any sibling or parent

mcqs.explanation: L-attributed definitions restrict inherited attributes to depend only on attributes of left siblings and parents, making them suitable for left-to-right evaluation.

mcqs.question: What is the main purpose of constructing a dependency graph in SDDs?

options.text: To visualize the parse tree structure
To determine the order of attribute evaluation

mcqs.explanation: Dependency graphs show the flow of information between attributes and help determine a valid order for evaluating the attributes without circular dependencies.

mcqs.question: Which of the following SDD types can handle both synthesized and inherited attributes with restrictions?
mcqs.explanation: L-attributed definitions can use both synthesized and inherited attributes, but with the restriction that inherited attributes can only depend on left siblings and parents.
